businessModel.text: Revenue from Consumer & Community Banking (retail deposits, cards, auto loans, 40%), Corporate & Investment Bank (advisory, markets, lending, 35%), and Asset & Wealth Management (advisory fees, AUM-based, 25%).

recentCatalysts.text: Full-year 2025 net income ~$55bn; ROTCE consistently above 17% NII (Net Interest Income) guidance raised Q4 2025 to $91.5bn+ for FY25 Investment-banking fees rebounded Q1 2025 (+26% YoY) on M&A and ECM activity BlackRock partnership deepens wealth-management cross-sell (AUM $4.3tn)
items: Full-year 2025 net income ~$55bn; ROTCE consistently above 17%
NII (Net Interest Income) guidance raised Q4 2025 to $91.5bn+ for FY25
Investment-banking fees rebounded Q1 2025 (+26% YoY) on M&A and ECM activity

keyRisks.text: 1. Tariff and recession risk could trigger loan-loss provision build in 2H 2026 2. Jamie Dimon announced retirement — succession risk creates governance premium 3. NII is duration-sensitive: Fed rate cuts compress NIM faster than modelled 4. Consumer-credit card charge-offs rising off COVID-era lows (now ~2.8%) 5. Russian/Chinese market exposure: sanctions compliance costs and reputational risk

risks.risk: 1. Tariff and recession risk could trigger loan-loss provision build in 2H 2026
2. Jamie Dimon announced retirement — succession risk creates governance premium
3. NII is duration-sensitive: Fed rate cuts compress NIM faster than modelled
4. Consumer-credit card charge-offs rising off COVID-era lows (now ~2.8%)
5. Russian/Chinese market exposure: sanctions compliance costs and reputational risk

whoShouldOwn.text: Quality-focused investors seeking above-peer ROE with superior franchise durability. Dividend yield (~2.3%) plus buyback provides dual return mechanism. NOT suitable for investors expecting rapid balance-sheet de-levering or those wanting pure investment-bank beta.

entryExit.text: Add on intraday dips below $295 (10% from 52-week low). HOLD $295–$320. REDUCE above $320. Watch: NII quarterly guidance vs consensus for rate sensitivity. | BUY: Add on intraday dips below $295 (10% from 52-week low). | REDUCE above $320.

sources.marketData: Live quote data sourced via DYOR HQ data pipeline (Google Sheets + Yahoo Finance). Fields include price, market capitalisation, 52-week range, P/E, EPS, and volume.
sources.companyFilings: Public company filings, regulatory announcements, investor presentations, and RNS where referenced in the analysis.
